title: Chopper Steps Toward Battle
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on chopper steps toward battle, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: Chopper Steps Toward Battle. Luffy understands the meaning of Hiriluk’s flag and refuses to let Wapol treat it like a decoration. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Looks like that kid of mine,

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Muhahaha! too bad for you but while we're here,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: You won't lay a finger on his majesty!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Quite the curious oddity, aren't you.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: You must be the abominable snowman the Villagers were making a fuss about long ago.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: How pitiful, living a wretched existence, scorned by all...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: What possible reason could a friendless monster like you have in wanting to save this kingdom!?

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Shut up! I can fight just fine even without anybody on my side!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: As long as Doc's flag is waving behind my back, I...

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: Not as long as I'm around!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Think you can handle those two?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I can take care of'em without breaking a sweat!

summary: This part highlights chopper steps toward battle and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
